title: 10 best herbal creams for bursitis

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, pain, and stiffness associated with bursitis, allowing for faster recovery and relief.
Pain-relieving: The cream's analgesic and anesthetic properties numb the affected area, providing instant relief from sharp pains and aches that often accompany bursitis.
Antioxidant: Arnica's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation by neutralizing free radicals, promoting healing and preventing further tissue damage.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica montana, also known as mountain arnica, creams helps with bursitis because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain. The cream's active ingredients, including arnicoside and flavonoids, work to soothe inflamed tissues and promote healing. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate pain and inflammation associated with bursitis, a condition characterized by inflammation of the fluid-filled sacs that cushion joints. By applying the cream topically, individuals can experience relief from bursitis symptoms and improve their overall quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds, particularly heliatriene, help with bursitis by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and pain associated with the condition.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Arnica montana, such as kaempferol and quercetin, possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which aid in soothing and healing bursitis-affected areas.

Thymoquinone: This sesquiterpene compound in Arnica montana has been found to possess analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which help in alleviating pain, reducing inflammation, and promoting healing in bursitis cases.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Arnica montana are widely used to make creams for bursitis due to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: The roots of Arnica montana are also used to make creams for bursitis, as they contain a high concentration of arnica oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Arnica montana are utilized to make creams for bursitis, as they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh arnica montana flowers on a sunny day in mid-summer.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to prevent spoilage.
Infuse 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil at 100°f for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth and add 1/4 cup of beeswax to 1 cup of infused oil for thickening.
Whip the mixture until light and fluffy then pour into jars for storage and use within 6 months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with bursitis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold cream can help protect the affected area from further damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing scar tissue formation that can worsen bursitis symptoms.

Wound-healing agents: Pot marigold has been traditionally used to accelerate wound healing, which is beneficial for bursitis as it helps promote collagen synthesis, improves skin elasticity, and reduces the risk of infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with bursitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, soothe and calm inflamed tissues, reducing pain and swelling associated with bursitis. Additionally, calendula's antiseptic properties prevent infection and promote healing in the affected area. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, calendula cream provides effective relief from bursitis symptoms, promoting faster recovery and reducing discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds in Calendula officinalis have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with bursitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): Acting as potent antioxidants, flavonoids in Calendula officinalis help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in bursitis by neutralizing free radicals and modulating the immune response.

Calendulin (a triterpene glycoside): Calendulin has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, which contributes to the reduction of pain and inflammation in bursitis.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most used part of Calendula officinalis due to their high concentration of flavonoids, triterpenoids, and carotenoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

Leaves: The leaves are another commonly used part of Calendula officinalis for bursitis creams due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenes, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.

Seeds: The seeds of Calendula officinalis are often used to create creams for bursitis as they contain high levels of fatty acids and antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair.
remedy_recipe: Infuse one ounce of dried calendula officinalis flowers in two cups of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes to create a tea.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl to remove solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat to melt.
Combine the cooled tea with 1/2 cup of shea butter and 1/4 cup of vitamin e oil in the bowl to create a mixture.
Whip the mixture in a stand mixer until it thickens and forms a smooth, consistent cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Many herbal creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like arnica, chamomile, and turmeric, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with bursitis.
Pain-relieving agents: Herbal creams containing capsaicin, wintergreen oil, or menthol can provide temporary pain relief by blocking pain receptors or reducing inflammation.
Antioxidants: Creams rich in antioxidants like vitamin E, aloe vera, and green tea may help protect the skin from oxidative stress and promote healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell pepper, creams helps with bursitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The creams contain capsaicin, a natural compound that blocks the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. This leads to a reduction in swelling and pain in the affected area. Additionally, capsaicin has been shown to increase blood flow, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing. As a result, applying Capsicum annuum creams can provide relief from bursitis symptoms, such as pain and stiffness.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: It helps reduce inflammation and pain in bursitis by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins and substance P, which are mediators of pain and inflammation.
Quercetin: It acts as an anti-inflammatory agent by suppressing the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and lipoxygenase, thereby reducing swelling and pain in the affected bursae.
Catechins: They possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area, thereby providing relief from bursitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used because they contain capsaicin, a potent analgesic compound that helps relieve pain associated with bursitis.
Leaves: They are used because they also contain capsaicin, which can be extracted and used in creams to reduce inflammation and pain in bursitis.
Seeds: They are used because they contain a higher concentration of capsaicin than the fruits, making them a more potent ingredient in creams for bursitis relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 lb of ripe capsicum annuum fruits and wash them thoroughly with cool water.
Steam the fruits for 20 minutes to break down the cell walls and release the active compounds.
Mix 1 cup of the steamed capsicum annuum with 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes or until it reaches 160°f to 180°f temperature.
Strain the mixture and pour it into sterile jars for storage and use within 6 months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: St John's Wort creams contain flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with bursitis.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in St John's Wort creams can help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by chronic inflammation, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further injury.

Pain-relieving properties: Hyperforin, a compound found in St John's Wort, has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, helping to relieve pain and discomfort associated with bursitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with bursitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The hypericin and hyperforin in the cream reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, while also preventing bacterial infection. The cream's soothing and calming effects also aid in the reduction of inflammation and promote the healing of damaged tissue, allowing the body to repair and recover from bursitis more effectively. This natural remedy provides relief from bursitis symptoms in a gentle and non-invasive manner.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This prenylated phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in bursitis-affected areas.

Naphthodianthrones (Hypericin and Pseudohypericin): These compounds possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to the reduction of inflammation and relief from bursitis symptoms.

Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and promote healing in bursitis-affected joints and tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Hypericum perforatum are used to make creams for bursitis because of their anti-inflammatory properties and flavonoid content.
Flowers: The flowers of Hypericum perforatum are used to make creams for bursitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Roots: The roots of Hypericum perforatum are used to make creams for bursitis because of their high concentration of hyperforin, which has anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 100 grams of base cream.
Combine 25 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers with 100 grams of base cream in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60-70 degrees celsius for 10-15 minutes to infuse the flowers.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the flowers from the cream.
Fill sterilized containers with the strained cream and allow it to cool before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, reduce swelling and pain in affected joints, providing relief from bursitis symptoms.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic properties of lavender, particularly its ability to inhibit the production of prostaglandins (pain-causing hormones), help alleviate pain associated with bursitis.
Antioxidant: Lavender's antioxidant activity helps protect the affected area from oxidative stress and free radical damage, which can exacerbate bursitis symptoms and slow down healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with bursitis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The cream's calming effects also help to relax the surrounding muscles, reducing tension and promoting healing. By applying the cream topically, individuals can experience relief from bursitis symptoms, including pain, stiffness, and reduced mobility. This natural remedy offers a gentle and non-invasive approach to managing bursitis.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which helps reduce pain and inflammation associated with bursitis.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which helps prevent infection and reduce inflammation in bursitis.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in bursitis, promoting healing and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with bursitis.
Flowers: Used for their calming and anti-inflammatory effects, which can aid in soothing bursitis symptoms.
Seeds: Contain compounds that possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them useful in reducing pain and inflammation caused by bursitis.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g dried lavender flowers in 1l water at 80c for 30-45 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid using a cheesecloth to obtain 800ml of lavender extract.
Melt 200g of beeswax in a double boiler and heat to 40c.
Combine 200g of shea butter with 100g of coconut oil in a separate container.
Mix the melted beeswax with the shea butter and coconut oil mixture then add 50g of lavender extract.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sweet briar contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with bursitis.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in sweet briar cream help protect the affected area from oxidative stress, reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair.
Wound healing properties: Sweet briar's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds also aid in wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and enhancing tissue regeneration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosa rubiginosa, also known as sweet briar, creams helps with bursitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herb's extracts have been traditionally used to reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healing in inflamed joints and soft tissues. The creams' soothing and cooling effects can help calm irritated areas, while its antimicrobial properties can help prevent infection and promote a faster recovery. This makes Rosa rubiginosa creams a popular natural remedy for managing bursitis symptoms. Regular use can provide relief and improve overall joint health.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in bursitis-infected areas.
Flavonoids (Quercetin): Quercetin is a flavonoid that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate bursitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation associated with bursitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used in creams for bursitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Used in creams for bursitis due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
Stems: Used in creams for bursitis due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of rosa rubiginosa flowers at peak bloom to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Infuse the flowers in 500ml of cold-pressed carrier oil for 2-3 weeks to extract their medicinal properties.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container to separate the solids from the liquid.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 20g of cocoa butter into the infused oil to create a stable and nourishing cream base.
Whip the mixture until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency then transfer it to a glass jar.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Elder cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids, saponins, and terpenes, which reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area.

Antioxidant activity: Elder cream's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate bursitis symptoms like pain and stiffness.

Analgesic and antispasmodic properties: Herbal elder creams often contain analgesics (pain-relievers) and antispasmodics that can help alleviate acute pain and reduce muscle spasms associated with bursitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, creams helps with bursitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream is made from the extract of the elder plant, which contains compounds that have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in inflamed joints. The active ingredients, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing. This makes Sambucus nigra cream a popular natural remedy for alleviating bursitis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with bursitis, making Sambucus nigra creams a potential remedy for this condition.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate the swelling and discomfort experienced in bursitis, promoting a faster recovery.
Ellagic acid: This phenolic acid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate bursitis symptoms, including pain, redness, and swelling.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate bursitis pain and swelling.
Barks: They are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce bursitis pain and inflammation.
Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce bursitis pain and prevent further inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of sambucus nigra flowers and leaves on a sunny day in june or july.
Infuse 50g of the harvested flowers and leaves in 250ml of oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 100g of the infused oil in a double boiler at 160-180°f.
Whip the cooled mixture until it thickens and has a creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal littleleaf linden creams can help reduce inflammation associated with bursitis, allowing for improved mobility and pain relief.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in these creams can help protect the affected area from further damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing scarring.
Analgesic: The cream's analgesic properties can help numb the area, providing temporary pain relief and making it easier to manage discomfort caused by bursitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tilia cordata, also known as littleleaf linden, creams helps with bursitis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream is often made from the plant's leaves and flowers, which contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in affected joints. By applying the cream topically, it can help to alleviate the symptoms of bursitis, such as pain, swelling, and stiffness, providing relief and promoting healing in the affected area. This natural approach can also help to reduce reliance on pain medication.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with bursitis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and modulating immune responses.

Terpenes (specifically, limonoids): Terpenes, such as limonoids found in Tilia cordata, may contribute to the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of the plant, helping to soothe and calm bursitis symptoms.
Tannins: Tannins, a type of polyphenol present in Tilia cordata, may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with bursitis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce pain and swelling associated with bursitis.
Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help relieve pain and discomfort caused by bursitis.
Buds: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of tilia cordata leaves and flowers to meet your desired needs.
Dry the harvested plant material in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to preserve its properties.
Create a herbal infusion by steeping 2 tablespoons of dried tilia cordata in 1 cup of boiling water.
Combine the herbal infusion with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency that is ready to use.

remedy_properties: Methyl salicylate: This property helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with bursitis, as methyl salicylate is a natural anti-inflammatory compound that works similarly to aspirin.
Glycosides: Wintergreen contains glycosides, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain, swelling, and stiffness caused by bursitis.

Tannins: The tannins present in wintergreen creams help constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and inflammation associated with bursitis, while also providing a soothing effect on the affected area.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as wintergreen, creams helps with bursitis because it contains methyl salicylate, a natural anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin. This compound reduces pain and inflammation in the affected area, providing relief from the discomfort and swelling associated with bursitis. Additionally, the cream's soothing properties help to calm irritated tissue, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of further irritation. As a result, wintergreen creams are often used to alleviate bursitis symptoms and promote overall joint health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, such as aucubin, help with bursitis by reducing inflammation and pain through their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid, contribute to the relief of bursitis symptoms by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, thus reducing swelling and pain.

Essential oils (terpenes): Essential oils, including borneol and bornyl acetate, help alleviate bursitis symptoms by acting as anti-inflammatory agents, reducing pain and inflammation through their topical application.
remedy_parts: Roots: High in glycosides, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating bursitis.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and volatile oils, they help reduce inflammation and promote healing in bursitis.
Barks: Containing salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, they help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in bursitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems fresh from the wild in late summer.
Dry the harvested gaultheria procumbens in a single layer at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 1-2 hours.
Infuse 1 cup of dried gaultheria procumbens in 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused gaultheria procumbens oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter to create the final cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation and alleviates pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, making it effective in relieving bursitis symptoms.

Antioxidant: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue degeneration associated with bursitis.
Pain-relieving: Turmeric contains compounds that have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with bursitis without the need for pharmaceuticals.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with bursitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric cream reduces inflammation and swelling in the affected area, providing relief from pain and discomfort. It also promotes healing and reduces oxidative stress, which can contribute to bursitis. Furthermore, turmeric's natural antiseptic properties help prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for tissue repair, making it an effective natural remedy for bursitis sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and pain in bursitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A bioactive compound that helps suppress the activity of enzymes responsible for inflammation and pain, thereby alleviating bursitis symptoms.
Turmerones: A group of sesquiterpenes that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of bursitis-affected joints.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of curcumin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are also used to make creams due to their curcumin content, which helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with bursitis.
Stems: The stems are occasionally used, but their primary role is to support the growth of the plant, and they may contain some curcumin, although less than the rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 500g of base cream typically made from coconut oil and shea butter, heated until melted.
Combine 200g of curcuma longa powder with 100g of beeswax and stir until well combined.
Mix the powder mixture into the heated base cream and stir continuously for 5 minutes.
Add 50g of vitamin e oil and 20g of essential oil and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into containers and refrigerate for 2-3 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry creams helps with bursitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The extract from highbush cranberries contains powerful compounds that reduce swelling and ease pain in affected joints, making it an effective natural remedy for treating bursitis. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties help to moisturize and soothe irritated skin, providing relief from discomfort and stiffness associated with bursitis. As a result, highbush cranberry creams offer a safe and natural way to alleviate symptoms and promote healing in those affected by this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Highbush cranberries contain a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with bursitis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in highbush cranberry creams may help protect the affected joint from further damage by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby promoting healing and recovery.

Wound-healing properties: Highbush cranberries have been traditionally used to aid wound healing, which can be beneficial for bursitis as it can promote the repair of damaged tissue and reduce scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with bursitis.
Barks: Contain astringent properties that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in affected joints.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries and grind them into a fine powder.
Combine the powdered vaccinium macrocarpon with 200 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an immersion blender or whisk.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, allowing the cream to mature and thicken over time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with bursitis because they possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that effectively alleviate pain and swelling in affected joints. The cream's active compounds, such as fenchone and anethole, penetrate deep into the skin to target the inflamed bursa sacs, reducing inflammation and discomfort. As a result, patients experience rapid relief from symptoms like stiffness, redness, and warmth, allowing them to resume their daily activities with ease and confidence.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with bursitis.

Pain-relieving glycosides: The presence of glycosides in fennel, such as anethole, has been shown to possess analgesic and anti-pyretic properties, making it effective in reducing bursitis-related pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant activity: Fennel's antioxidant compounds can help combat oxidative stress and inflammation, which are common underlying factors of bursitis, and may aid in the repair and healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, fennel roots help reduce swelling and pain associated with bursitis.
Barks: Containing bioactive flavonoids, fennel barks have been traditionally used to alleviate joint inflammation and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried fennel seeds at dawn and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground fennel powder with 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler and melt for 30 minutes over low heat.
Add 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil to the melted wax mixture and stir until fully incorporated within 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to 60 degrees celsius, then add 2 grams of vitamin e oil and mix thoroughly for 1 minute.
Pour the cooled mixture into a sterilized container and allow it to solidify at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with bursitis because they offer a natural, soothing approach to reducing inflammation and alleviating pain. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, penetrate deep into the affected area, calming swollen joints and tendons. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals, which can exacerbate bursitis symptoms. By applying a topical cream containing chamomile extract, individuals with bursitis can experience relief from discomfort and stiffness, allowing for improved mobility and reduced swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and pain associated with bursitis by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidant activity helps protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue degeneration in bursitis.

Soothing and calming property: Chamomile's soothing and calming effects help reduce pain, discomfort, and stiffness associated with bursitis, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which can exacerbate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin and other bioactive compounds, which help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with bursitis.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, these flowers possess anti-inflammatory properties that can effectively soothe and calm irritated joints and tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers carefully ensuring no contamination or adulteration.
Dry the harvested flowers in shade for 7-10 days or using desiccant machines according to manufacturer's instructions.
Combine dried flowers with coconut oil and beeswax at a ratio of 3:1 by weight, then heat mixture gently until beeswax melts.
Strain the cooled mixture through cheesecloth or coffee filters to remove solid particles, filtering until clear consistency is achieved.
Fill sterilized containers with the final cream, storing in refrigerator for up to 6 months while keeping away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony creams helps with bursitis because of their potent anti-inflammatory properties. The natural herbs and botanicals in these creams, such as peony root and licorice root, work to reduce swelling and ease pain in affected joints. By penetrating deep into the skin, these ingredients help to block the production of inflammatory chemicals, calming irritated tissues and promoting relaxation of tense muscles. As a result, Chinese peony creams can provide fast and effective relief from bursitis symptoms, allowing for improved mobility and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peony's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling, pain, and stiffness associated with bursitis by reducing prostaglandins and leukotrienes, which contribute to inflammation.
Analgesic: The analgesic effects of peony can provide temporary relief from pain caused by bursitis, allowing for a more comfortable range of motion and reduced discomfort.

Antioxidant: Peony's antioxidant properties help protect the affected area from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate inflammation and chronic conditions like bursitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Chinese Peony plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with bursitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the plant contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort caused by bursitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh paeonia lactiflora flowers at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours or until crispy.
Combine 20g of dried paeonia lactiflora flowers with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil and infuse for 4 days.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard solids after 2 hours of infusion time.
Mix the strained liquid with 10g of beeswax and 20g of cocoa butter and heat gently until melted and smooth, stirring constantly for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with bursitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as silymarin and flavonoids, work to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the affected joints. Additionally, milk thistle's antioxidant properties help protect against tissue damage caused by chronic inflammation, promoting a healthy environment for joint repair and healing. By targeting the underlying causes of bursitis, milk thistle creams can provide effective relief from symptoms, improving range of motion and overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Milk thistle creams contain silymarin, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with bursitis.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in milk thistle creams help protect the affected area from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.

Immune-modulating property: Milk thistle has immunomodulatory effects, which means it can help regulate the body's immune response to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair, making it an effective treatment for bursitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a flavonoid complex that helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with bursitis.
Seeds: Contain antioxidant-rich fatty acids and omega-3 fatty acids that help soothe and reduce inflammation in affected joints.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality silybum marianum leaves and flowers at dawn on a dry day.
Dry the plant material in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days.
Grind dried silybum marianum into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered herb with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 5 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the cream has cooled to around 38°c.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with bursitis because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the affected area, reducing swelling and pain. The natural compounds present in aloe vera gel, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, work to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of aloe vera help to hydrate the skin, promoting healthy tissue growth and repair, which can aid in the healing process of bursitis. This natural remedy provides relief from the discomfort and stiffness associated with bursitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and pain associated with bursitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich composition of herbal aloe creams neutralizes free radicals that contribute to tissue damage and inflammation in bursitis, promoting healing and reducing oxidative stress.

Moisturizing and soothing effects: Aloe vera's hydrating and soothing properties help calm irritated skin and reduce redness associated with bursitis, providing relief from discomfort and improving overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the aloe plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and swelling associated with bursitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of aloe have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing bursitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and clean them thoroughly with distilled water to remove impurities.
Extract the aloe vera gel using a blender or juicer and mix it with 10 grams of turmeric powder.
Combine the extracted aloe vera gel mixture with 20 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax pellets.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 65°c for 15 minutes while stirring occasionally to ensure even melting.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool and solidify before applying to affected areas.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties, which come from the presence of compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with bursitis.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of Kansas coneflower cream help to protect the affected area from further damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.

Analgesic and antispasmodic effects: The cream's analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which come from the presence of compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with bursitis, while also relaxing muscle spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with bursitis.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve joint pain and stiffness caused by bursitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia flowers, ensuring proper identification and handling.
Combine 100 grams of echinacea flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler.
Infuse the mixture for at least 4 hours or overnight, allowing the herbal properties to extract into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Mix the filtered oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil, heating until melted and blending thoroughly.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with bursitis because they harness the anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties of Echinacea pallida, a plant native to the Great Plains. The cream's active compounds, such as echinacein and glycosides, penetrate deep into the affected area, reducing swelling and soothing joint pain. By targeting the root cause of bursitis, Kansas coneflower creams provide natural relief from stiffness, inflammation, and discomfort, allowing individuals to resume their normal activities without compromise.

American witch hazel creams helps with bursitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate pain in affected joints. The astringent qualities of witch hazel also help to reduce inflammation by constricting blood vessels, thereby reducing fluid buildup around the joint. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to prevent infection and promote healing. By applying topical creams containing witch hazel to the affected area, individuals with bursitis can experience relief from discomfort and improved mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The astringent and anti-inflammatory properties of witch hazel help reduce swelling and pain associated with bursitis by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid buildup.
Antiseptic: Witch hazel's antiseptic properties help prevent infection by inhibiting the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause inflammation and further exacerbate bursitis symptoms.
Pain-relieving: The topical analgesic properties of witch hazel help numb the area and reduce pain associated with bursitis, providing temporary relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, witch hazel roots are effective in reducing inflammation and soothing pain associated with bursitis.
Barks: The outer layer of the stem, witch hazel bark contains astringent compounds that help to reduce swelling and promote healing in affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality hamamelis virginiana leaf and bark, calendula flowers, and beeswax for the cream's formula.
Combine 10 grams of dried hamamelis virginiana herb, 5 grams of dried calendula flowers, and 20ml of carrier oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Infuse the mixture with gentle heat over low heat for 30 minutes to extract the herbal active compounds from the plants.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the herbal-infused oil.
Combine 10 grams of beeswax, 20ml of herbal-infused oil, and 10ml of distilled water in a double boiler, stirring until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red shiso creams helps with bursitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain in affected joints. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thus alleviating inflammation. Additionally, red shiso cream's moisturizing properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing friction and irritation that can exacerbate bursitis symptoms. By addressing both inflammation and discomfort, red shiso creams provide a natural and effective solution for managing bursitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Red shiso cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like carnosic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with bursitis, allowing for faster healing and relief.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of red shiso cream help protect the affected area from oxidative stress, reducing damage to tissues and promoting a healthy environment for recovery.

Pain-relieving terpenes: Red shiso cream contains pain-relieving terpenes like limonene and β-pinene, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and pain associated with bursitis, providing quick relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with bursitis.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of glycosides and phenolic acids, rhyzomes provide anti-inflammatory properties and analgesic relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lithospermum erythrorhizon roots weighing approximately 250 grams.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place for seven to ten days or until crispy.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for thirty minutes.
Mix one part of the powdered lithospermum erythrorhizon with three parts of beeswax and five parts of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at a low heat for twenty-five to thirty minutes or until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with bursitis because they promote relaxation and reduce inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties of valerian root help to soothe irritated joints, reducing pain and stiffness associated with bursitis. Additionally, the calming effects of valerian cream can also help to alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for exacerbating bursitis symptoms. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal valerian creams offer a natural solution for managing bursitis discomfort and promoting overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Valerian's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with bursitis, making it easier to manage symptoms.
Antioxidant: Valerian's antioxidant properties protect the affected joint from further damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy healing and reducing the risk of chronic inflammation.

Pain-relieving: Valerian contains compounds that have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with bursitis, allowing for improved mobility and quality of life.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and sesquiterpenes, which provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for soothing bursitis pain.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of valerenic acid, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making it an ideal component for bursitis creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots and dry them thoroughly for at least two weeks to preserve potency.
Combine one part dried valeriana officinalis root with three parts coconut oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 hours or overnight allowing the active compounds to infuse into the oil.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids and transferring liquid to an airtight container.
Mix equal parts of the infused oil with beeswax and heat gently until melted then pour into containers and let cool and solidify at room temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for bursitis are topical ointments infused with natural herbs that help alleviate bursitis symptoms. These creams contain anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that reduce swelling and discomfort in affected joints. Examples include creams containing arnica, turmeric, and capsaicin, which have been shown to improve lives by providing relief from chronic pain and inflammation, allowing individuals to engage in daily activities and maintain their quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bursitis that are treated with herbal creams are tendinitis, tenosynovitis, and arthritis. These conditions involve inflammation or irritation of tendons, ligaments, or joints, leading to pain and stiffness. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as arnica, capsaicin, and devil's claw can help reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote healing in these areas.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with bursitis is a blend of arnica, calendula, and St. John's Wort. Arnica reduces inflammation and relieves pain, while calendula promotes healing and soothes the skin. St. John's Wort, with its antiseptic properties, helps to prevent infection and promote a quick recovery. Applying a topical cream containing these herbs can provide effective relief from bursitis symptoms, reducing swelling and promoting a faster healing process. Regular use may also help prevent future episodes.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Planta medica, herbal creams containing extracts from plants such as Commiphora incisa and Commiphora mukul can be beneficial for bursitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties. The study, which involved testing the aqueous extracts of the resins of these plants on carrageenan-induced rat paw edema, found that the octanordammarane triterpene, mansumbinoic acid, isolated from the resin of C. incisa, exhibited a significant anti-inflammatory action. Specifically, mansumbinoic acid was able to reverse an established inflammatory response when administered 2 hours after induction, suggesting its potential in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with bursitis.
